Details:
Effective removal (69 to 97 percent) of alachlor by ozone was observed during bench- and pilot-scale studies utilizing both ground and surface waters. The ozone dose needed to achieve these removal percentage is very high (7 to 28 mg/L) compared to typical doses used in drinking water treatment [721][728].
Moderate removal (70 percent) is achieved at low ozone doses (4.3 mg/L). Higher removal (80 to 90 percent) can be achieved when ozone application is combined alum and/or PAC addition [723].
The effect of ozonation can be enhanced by the use of metal catalysts such as Fe+2, Mn+2, and Cu/Al2O3 [716]. Alachlor degradation was observed to increase as the pH increased.
